headline: South Florida's Week of Luxury Peaks & Airport Upgrades

stories.title: Luxury Home Sales Hit All-Time Highs Across South Florida
stories.summary: Million-dollar home sales in South Florida are absolutely crushing it—$1M+ properties in Miami-Dade and Broward jumped 21% and 14% YoY in Jan, while Palm Beach tops everyone with luxury prices up 67.8% since 2019. Tech billionaires like Larry Page and Mark Zuckerberg are lowkey driving demand by relocating to the region.
stories.takeaway: Wealthy buyers from Cali and NYC are flooding South Florida, making luxury real estate fr the hottest market in the country rn.

stories.title: Wahlburgers Opens at Sarasota Airport, SRQ Completes Major Concourse Upgrade
stories.summary: Wahlburgers (Wahlberg brothers' burger chain) officially opened at Sarasota-Bradenton International Airport on Feb 28 as part of a massive $16M+ food & beverage overhaul in Concourse B. SRQ is already eyeing TSA guest passes to bring in non-travelers.
stories.takeaway: SRQ's upgraded concourse brings legit national dining brands to Gulf Coast travelers and could soon let anyone hop into the airport to eat without a ticket.

stories.title: Palm Beach Boat Show Set to Draw 55K+ Visitors, Showcase $1.2B+ in Yachts
stories.summary: The 44th annual Palm Beach International Boat Show kicks off March 25-29 with over 600 exhibitors, 1,000+ boats, and a record lineup of superyachts worth $1.2B. The show's expected to generate ~$1.05B in statewide economic impact.
stories.takeaway: PBIBS is basically South Florida's biggest flex for luxury tourism, bringing insane foot traffic and yacht sales during prime spring season.

stories.title: ServiceNow Expanding to Downtown West Palm Beach with 850+ High-Paying Jobs
stories.summary: Tech giant ServiceNow just announced it's opening a regional headquarters in downtown West Palm Beach by 2028, bringing over 850 high-paying positions to the area. Part of the broader wave of tech companies relocating to South Florida.
stories.takeaway: Another major tech player choosing South Florida tbh shows the region's becoming legitimate competition to Silicon Valley for talent & innovation.

watchNext.title: Florida Legislature's Special Budget Session Could Reshape Tax Policy
watchNext.why: Florida's legislature adjourned mid-March with budget negotiations still pending, with a special session planned for mid-April. DeSantis is also pushing property tax reforms that could impact real estate values & development.

watchNext.title: Fort Lauderdale Downtown Economy Surges 20% Since 2022, Tech Hub Emerging
watchNext.why: Downtown Fort Lauderdale's economic output has grown 20% recently & is becoming a legit tech hub with projects like FAT Village & Napster HQ. Worth watching as the 'Wall Street of the South' narrative solidifies.
